summary: The Slock UX implementation successfully delivers the card-based UI, multi-agent discussion UX, memory display, and management interactions requested. It effectively uses Feishu elements (collapsible_panel, note, standard_icon) to create responsive, well-structured interfaces. The color coding and visual hierarchy are clear. I've noted a few minor aesthetic and interaction improvements that could further enhance the mobile experience.

suggestions.evidence: src/slock_engine/card_templates.py:1015-1020, build_discussion_expand_card uses '👥 {' · '.join(participants[:2])} 等 {len(participants)} 人' which works but could be tighter on mobile.
suggestions.recommendation: Consider shortening the participant list in headers to just the count on very narrow mobile screens, e.g. using `f'👥 {len(participants)}人参与'` if screen width is constrained, though Feishu handles this reasonably well.

suggestions.evidence: src/slock_engine/card_templates.py:932, _SPEAKER_COLORS array is used for both live discussion and expanded view.
suggestions.recommendation: The use of Feishu note elements with standard_icon tokens colored per speaker is an excellent, compact way to represent conversation. It works very well on both desktop and mobile.

suggestions.evidence: src/slock_engine/card_templates.py:2104-2134, build_resolved_escalation_card converts action buttons into a simple status label after resolution.
suggestions.recommendation: Replacing buttons with a timestamped status label (✅ 已解决) is a great pattern that prevents duplicate actions and clarifies state. It keeps the chat history clean.
